author: Trevor Bradford, IDEA Brand Consultancy (Brandvoice Retail Ltd)
supersedes: All previous skill lists and skill architecture documents. This is the authoritative version.
governing_principle: Every skill exists to help a brand owner understand why their customer is not buying and what to fix first. We fix a measurable commercial problem (conversion), not sell brand strategy. The science is the engine, not the pitch.
source_documents: IDEA_Avatar2_Dev_Brief_v1.0.docx (IDEA-AV2-DEV-001 v1.0) — three-layer Avatar 2.0 + Four Buyer States

tiers.name: Foundation
tiers.function: Powers everything. In context on every session. The lens through which all other skills are applied.

tiers.name: Diagnostic pipeline
tiers.function: The analytical engine. Runs in sequence; each skill feeds the next. Produces the Avatar profile, buyer state classification, and Trust Gap Score.
tiers.sequence_rule: 07 runs first (corpus). 04 then 05 then 06; 05 (internal) must classify the dominant buyer state before 06 and 09 run. 08 enriches the S2 stage within 04.

tiers.name: Output and action
tiers.function: Translates diagnostic findings into things the user can act on immediately: Decision Trigger, design brief, CAPTURE copy, listing analysis.

tiers.name: Science engine
tiers.function: The behavioural science and neuroscience foundation. Informs precision of all outputs. Never visible to the user.

skills.name: Bolte Taylor: Four Buyer States Foundation
Cialdini: The Six Principles of Influence
Lindstrom and Sutherland: Sensory and Irrational Value

scope_note: The master-architecture header states '14 Alpha skills · 6 Beta skills' but the per-skill scope table yields 17 Alpha / 3 Beta (Beta = 11, 13, 14). This manifest follows the granular per-skill table. DISCREPANCY to confirm with Trevor.
terminology_policy: IDEA-POLICY-TERM-001 v1.0 (2026-06-21) — three tiers. Tier A (Trust Gap™, Decision Trigger™, CAPTURE Framework™, Avatar 2.0™, the four pillar names) appears EVERYWHERE incl. app UI — it is the product's commercial distinctiveness; do not strip it. Tier B (The Assessor/Protector/Expresser/Connector, CAPTURE element names) appears on website/book and in app UI ONLY in opt-in expansion panels — never in primary output panels (Component 0, Avatar fields, placement instruction). Tier C (neuroanatomy, S1-S4 labels, field names, academic citations) NEVER appears in any user-facing surface. Supersedes the old blanket 'no framework terminology' rule.
hard_rules: Tier C engine internals — neuroanatomical framing, S1-S4 stage labels, field names (dominant_buyer_state, copy_calibration_direction), academic citations (Bolte Taylor, Zaltman, Kahneman, Cialdini, Shotton, Lindstrom, Sutherland) — must NEVER appear in any user-facing output. The science disappears; the precision remains.

Tier B buyer-state names (Assessor, Protector, Expresser, Connector) and CAPTURE element names must NOT appear in primary output panels (Component 0, Avatar profile fields, placement instruction); they may appear only in optional opt-in expansion panels.

Tier A proprietary commercial terms (Trust Gap™, Decision Trigger™, CAPTURE Framework™, Avatar 2.0™, the four pillar names) MUST appear consistently across all surfaces incl. the app UI — their absence breaks continuity with the website/marketing and fails the Distinctive pillar.
No confidence score / percentage / numeric certainty indicator on any output.
No trigger-selection dropdown — one trigger, one panel, one action. If the user picks their own trigger, the tool becomes a label maker.
Jobs-to-be-done / JTBD / 'struggling moment' is retired. Use 'Purchase motivation'.
Component 0 (primary conversion problem) contains no buyer-state names and no Tier C engine internals — plain commercial language only (Tier A terms are fine).
Demographics are not a field in the Avatar 2.0 forensic portrait — they live in the Brand Context profile.

Brand anchors for the six triggers are fixed: Permission=Harvard Medical School, Recognition=Dove, Identity=Apple, Belonging=Patagonia, Momentum=Amazon's Choice, Fear-of-Loss=FOMO signal. (Recognition corrected Lego→Dove per IDEA-APP-FIXES-001; Lego is the Identity anchor.)

The free Trust Gap diagnostic questions must be EVIDENCE-BASED (answerable by observing the listing/hero/bullets/reviews), not brand self-assessment — forensic entry point, not a confidence score (IDEA-APP-FIXES-001 Fix 1).
Use the trademark symbol on first use per surface: Decision Trigger™, Trust Gap™, CAPTURE Framework™, Avatar 2.0™.
Brand tokens: BRAND_BLUE #1A3557, ACCENT_GOLD #C9A84C, font Arial throughout.
reference_case: Guyology Labs — Trust Gap 62/100, primary gap Empathetic (8/25), dominant buyer state The Protector (internal), primary Decision Trigger Recognition.
